Summary: OLIG2 is a basic-helix-loop-helix transcription factor and regulates oligodendrocyte development in mouse NSCs, OPCs, and newly formed oligodendrocytes (NFOs). Knockout of Olig2 in mice led to failure of oligodendroglial lineage generation. Specific deletion of Olig2 in OPCs reduced oligodendrocyte differentiation and caused hypomyelination. Ablation of Olig2 in immature oligodendrocytes induced maturation and promoted myelination and remyelination. Overexpression of Olig2 in neural stem cells promoted generation of OPCs and mature oligodendrocytes. OLIG2 binds protein-coding and lncRNA loci. Some genes marked by both H3K27m3 and H3K4m3 bivalent marks were bound by OLIG2. Motif analysis indicated cooperation with other transcription factors and OLIG2 can both activate and repress lncRNAs enriched for oligodendrocyte differentiation. OLIG2 is involved in regulation of Olig2 after transcription via the Olig2 3' UTR, which contains AU-rich elements. In NSCs OLIG2 binds and represses lnc-OPC expression and this repression likely maintains NSC self-renewal. In OPCs OLIG2 did not bind lnc-OPC's upstream regulatory region and lnc-OPC expression increased. Knocking down lnc-OPC greatly reduced OPC formation from NSCs. Genome-wide OLIG2-bound lncRNAs clustered by expression, and OLIG2 may act as a repressor of a set of OLIG2-bound lncRNAs in NSCs (group 1, 39 lncRNAs) while OLIG2 may act as an activator of a set of OLIG2-bound lncRNAs in OPCs and NFOs (group 2, nine lncRNAs). Expression of those groups correlated with oligodendrocyte development and differentiation.
References: Wei, H., Dong, X., You, Y., Hai, B., Duran, R. C., Wu, X., Kharas, N., & Wu, J. Q. (2021). OLIG2 regulates lncRNAs and its own expression during oligodendrocyte lineage formation. BMC Biol, 19(1), 132. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12915-021-01057-6
